Tests the behavior of `Math.pow` with positive zero and a negative exponent.

# Purpose
This code is a single assertion statement that tests the behavior of the `Math.pow` function in JavaScript. It checks if raising positive zero (`+0`) to a negative exponent (`-5.2`) results in `Infinity`, which is the expected mathematical outcome. This assertion is likely part of a test suite to verify the correct implementation of the `Math.pow` function, ensuring that it handles edge cases involving zero and negative exponents correctly. The code provides narrow functionality focused on a specific mathematical operation.
